task: Using our insurance claims extract, estimate the annual incidence and point prevalence of type 2 diabetes for 2019-2023, with appropriate denominators.

rationale.health/code-mapping.md: Diabetes defined from versioned diagnosis code lists
rationale.health/phenotyping.md: Codes grouped into a case definition for the condition
rationale.health/secondary-data.md: RECORD data-source table and code-list reporting for claims
rationale.health/study-flow.md: Selection-from-database flow with counts for the cohort
rationale.analysis/sensitivity-analysis.md: Probe robustness to denominator and lookback choices

task: Fit a Cox model for mortality where the exposure is a medication that patients start and stop over follow-up, accounting for the time-varying nature of treatment.

rationale.analysis/regression-modeling.md: Survival regression modeling choices, splines, no dichotomizing
rationale.analysis/correlated-data.md: Within-subject dependence from repeated treatment records
rationale.analysis/model-assumptions.md: Proportional-hazards and residual checks for Cox
rationale.health/routinely-collected-data.md: Time-varying treatment risks immortal time; windows/episodes
rationale.analysis/sensitivity-analysis.md: Test robustness of the time-varying specification

task: Produce a baseline characteristics table comparing treated and untreated groups for the manuscript, with standardized differences.

rationale.health/clinical-tables.md: Table 1 with gtsummary, SMD not p-values, missingness
rationale.analysis/reporting.md: Labels-first, CIs, rounding underpin the table
rationale.analysis/missingness.md: Per-variable missingness must be shown in Table 1
rationale.health/reporting-standards.md: Table feeds the EQUATOR/STROBE manuscript checklist

task: Clean and standardize the ICD-10 diagnosis codes in our raw extract, then map ICD-9 records from earlier years onto the same grouping.

rationale.health/code-mapping.md: Code cleaning sequence, ICD-9 to ICD-10 crosswalk loss
rationale.analysis/data-integrity.md: Explicit character types, range/validity checks on codes
rationale.analysis/data-contracts.md: Gate on orphan/unmapped thresholds, block on failure
rationale.analysis/reproducibility.md: Freeze code-set versions, pin packages, transformation log

task: Several covariates in our cohort have missing values; set up a principled multiple-imputation workflow before we run the adjusted models.

rationale.analysis/missingness.md: Mechanism-aware handling, no silent deletion, MI design
rationale.analysis/regression-modeling.md: Imputation model must match the analysis model
rationale.analysis/sensitivity-analysis.md: Compare to complete-case; vary MAR assumption
rationale.analysis/reproducibility.md: Seed the imputation for reproducible draws

task: Write a reusable function to reshape our wide lab panel into long format and add unit tests for it.

rationale.analysis/incremental-functions.md: Abstracting into a reusable function, STOP-and-confirm gate
rationale.analysis/pseudocode-first.md: Pseudocode the reshape logic before coding
rationale.analysis/data-integrity.md: Reshape must preserve row accounting and types

task: Prepare the main results figures for journal submission — a forest plot of adjusted estimates and a survival-curve panel.

rationale.analysis/reporting.md: Estimates shown with CIs, consistent rounding
rationale.analysis/results-writing.md: Figure values/captions tie to inline code
rationale.analysis/numeric-computation.md: Plotted numbers computed by code, never by hand

task: Build a computable phenotype to identify patients with chronic kidney disease from EHR diagnoses, labs, and medications, then validate it.

rationale.health/phenotyping.md: Comprehension gate and PPV validation for the case algorithm
rationale.health/code-mapping.md: Diagnosis/lab/med codes are versioned inputs to the phenotype
rationale.health/routinely-collected-data.md: EHR time windows and episodes feed the definition
rationale.analysis/pseudocode-first.md: Phenotype logic pseudocoded before any code
rationale.analysis/diagrams.md: ASCII/decision diagrams required for the algorithm
rationale.analysis/sensitivity-analysis.md: Carry phenotype measurement error into sensitivity

task: Analyze our one-time community health survey to estimate the prevalence of hypertension and describe how it varies by age and sex.

rationale.analysis/statistical-reporting.md: Descriptive task sets methods + STROBE/descriptive framework
rationale.analysis/initial-data-analysis.md: Structured IDA before describing the survey
rationale.analysis/reporting.md: Prevalence reported with CIs, labels-first, no p in tables
rationale.analysis/missingness.md: Account for survey non-response and item missingness

task: Our primary effect estimate is in; now design a sensitivity analysis to check how robust it is to the outlier handling and the exposure-definition choices we made.

rationale.analysis/sensitivity-analysis.md: Pre-specify, reuse the pipeline, report alongside primary
rationale.analysis/outliers.md: Outlier handling is the variation being probed; document it
rationale.analysis/pre-specification.md: Sensitivity analyses should be pre-specified, flag post-hoc
rationale.analysis/reproducibility.md: Re-run the same seeded pipeline for each variant
